mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0% over a rolling 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and inflation indicates strong consumer demand pricing power, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity that suppresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal robust construction sector activity and consumer confidence, which immediately lifts market sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations over a rolling 2-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% imposes severe financing costs on leveraged corporate balance sheets, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0% over a 3-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output that directly increases corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.
